features.summary: Lilly reported Q1 2021 revenue growth of 16% (13% constant currency) driven entirely by volume growth of 17 percentage points. Core business grew 7% excluding COVID-19 antibodies and prior year inventory stocking. Key growth products now represent 52% of core business, with strong performance from Trulicity maintaining market leadership in GLP-1s. Operating margin was 27.5% (30.1% excluding FX impact), with foreign exchange creating significant headwinds on margins.
features.management_outlook: Management raised full-year revenue guidance by $100M to $26.6-27.6B while narrowing COVID antibody revenue range to $1.0-1.5B. They increased R&D spending guidance to $6.9-7.1B, including additional investments in donanemab Alzheimer's trials and COVID antibody development. The company expects continued top-line growth and margin expansion throughout 2021, with confidence in achieving mid-teens EPS growth and operating margin expansion to mid-to-high 30s longer-term.
features.risks: Foreign exchange impacts created over 250 basis points negative effect on gross and operating margins in Q1. COVID-19 continues to affect healthcare utilization and new therapy starts in several therapeutic classes. Pricing pressures persist, particularly from Taltz access improvements creating near-term headwinds. Regulatory uncertainty exists around donanemab's approval path, with FDA indicating current data insufficient for near-term submission based on single pivotal study.
features.key_drivers: Tirzepatide Phase III results exceeded expectations with superior A1C reduction and weight loss versus competitors, including doubling semaglutide's weight loss in head-to-head trial. The company initiated new Phase III Alzheimer's prevention trial (TRAILBLAZER-3) and expanded donanemab development program. Strong volume growth continues across key brands including Trulicity, Taltz, and Verzenio, with Taltz gaining significant market share despite pricing pressures from improved access.
